summary: The plan is architecturally sound and well-aligned with the existing codebase. The overlay-based approach correctly avoids polluting the ModeManager's mode rotation. Position data is already exposed by 4/5 systems, making the constellation layer's data access straightforward. Three conditions must be addressed before implementation.

conditions.title: Shader warp divergence across five systems is underspecified
conditions.description: The plan states the constellation vertex shader 'must apply the identical warp transform as the parent point system's vertex shader' and acknowledges different systems use different transforms (pointWarp uses FBM, particleWarp uses curl3, ribbonWarp adds sway, crystalWarp uses quantized noise, microGeometry is CPU-driven). The mitigation — 'a uniform flag can switch warp profiles per system type if needed' — is too vague for what is the plan's single highest-risk item. A constellation shader that replicates all five warp pipelines would be fragile and violate DRY. Recommend one of: (A) extract a shared warp function into a GLSL include and refactor existing shaders to use it, then constellation uses the same include; (B) accept that constellation lines use only the pointWarp transform as a 'common approximation' and document the visual drift on curl3/crystal/ribbon modes as acceptable; (C) rebuild line topology each frame from the GPU-deformed positions via transform feedback or readback (expensive, likely not worth it). The plan must commit to one strategy and document the tradeoff.

conditions.title: MicroGeometry position access is CPU-driven and incompatible with the proposed pattern
conditions.description: The plan proposes a PositionProvider interface returning Float32Array. Four systems (particleField, ribbonField, pointCloud, crystalField) already expose a `positions` getter returning Float32Array|null. However, MicroGeometry applies deformation on the CPU each frame via instance matrices — its basePositions are pre-deformation and the shader is entirely different (instanced mesh, not Points). The constellation vertex shader cannot apply instanceMatrix transforms to line endpoints. The plan must either: (A) exclude MicroGeometry from constellation overlay and document this as intentional, or (B) describe a separate line rendering path for instanced systems. Option A is strongly recommended given the architectural mismatch.

conditions.title: ParticleField position format mismatch
conditions.description: ParticleField exposes positions via `getParticlePositions()` returning `Array<{x,y}>` (2D, 0-1 normalized), not a Float32Array of 3D world-space positions. The constellation system needs world-space vec3 data. The plan's PositionProvider interface assumes uniform Float32Array access. Either ParticleField needs a `positions: Float32Array|null` getter added (returning world-space 3D coords from the BufferGeometry's position attribute), or the constellation system needs a ParticleField-specific adapter. Since the BufferGeometry already has a `position` attribute with world-space coords, reading directly from `geometry.getAttribute('position').array` is the cleanest path — but this means the PositionProvider should document that it returns base (pre-shader) world-space positions.

interface_impact.detail: Adding an optional `positions` accessor to GeometrySystem is a backward-compatible extension. Three systems (ribbonField, pointCloud, crystalField) already implement it. ParticleField and MicroGeometry need adaptation (see C2, C3). The proposed PositionProvider interface is unnecessary — extending GeometrySystem directly is cleaner and avoids a parallel interface hierarchy.

mode_manager_impact.detail: Adding overlay support to ModeManager is a meaningful extension to a core orchestration component (59 existing tests). The plan's approach — attachOverlay() that hooks into mode switches — is reasonable. Key concern: overlay must participate in the crossfade lifecycle (opacity tracking during transitions) without breaking the existing state machine. Recommend the overlay's draw() receive the active system's current opacity rather than managing its own transition state.

shader_system_impact.detail: Two new GLSL files plus a new entry in shaderRegistry is consistent with existing patterns. The constellation shaders must import noise3d.glsl via string concatenation (established pattern). ShaderErrorCollector will automatically capture compilation errors. The healthGate system should validate constellation attributes alongside existing systems — add the constellation system to initAllForValidation() or validate separately.

quality_system_impact.detail: Adding enableConstellationLines and maxConstellationSegments to QualityProfile follows the established pattern (cf. enableSparkle, enablePointerRepulsion). The tier thresholds (0/1500/3000) are reasonable given existing maxPoints caps (200/800/2000). No structural changes needed.

performance_impact.detail: O(n²) proximity at init for ≤2000 points is ~2M comparisons, acceptable. 3000 LineSegments = 6000 vertices is lightweight for modern GPUs. Static topology with uniform-only updates per frame adds negligible draw call overhead. The plan correctly caps connections both per-point and globally.

test_impact.detail: New test file for constellation system is self-contained. ModeManager tests (59 existing) should be extended to cover overlay attachment/detachment and mode-switch rebuild. HealthGate integration tests should include constellation system validation.

risks.risk: Line-point visual drift during audio-reactive deformation
risks.detail: If the constellation vertex shader does not perfectly replicate each system's unique deformation pipeline, lines will visibly detach from their anchor points during bass spikes. This is especially problematic for curl3-based (particleField) and quantized (crystalField) deformations where the warp character is fundamentally different from pointWarp.
risks.mitigation: See condition C1. Recommend accepting pointWarp-only approximation with documented visual drift.

risks.risk: Mode switch causes visible pop in constellation topology
risks.detail: When mode switches, constellation topology rebuilds from new positions. During crossfade, both old and new topologies should be visible with matching opacity. The plan's current description ('rebuilds on mode switch') suggests an instant swap rather than a crossfade of line topologies.
risks.mitigation: During transition, fade out old constellation topology with outgoing system opacity, rebuild from incoming system positions, fade in with incoming opacity. May require two active line meshes during transition.

strengths: Correct identification of overlay vs mode pattern — compositing over existing systems is the right architectural choice
Static topology with dynamic uniforms is an excellent performance decision that avoids per-frame CPU geometry rebuilds
Connection count bounding (per-point + global cap) prevents GPU budget overruns
Quality tier integration follows established patterns precisely
Shader material choice (LineSegments + custom ShaderMaterial) is the most efficient Three.js line primitive
weaknesses: Shader warp replication strategy is the plan's weakest point — acknowledges the problem but defers the solution
MicroGeometry compatibility is not addressed despite its fundamentally different rendering pipeline
Mode transition handling for constellation topology is underspecified
No mention of how healthGate validation will cover the new constellation system
